
A python solution exists in the following "windows 11" directory:

"C:\Users\AllanBarnard\OneDrive - Accelalpha Software Pvt. Ltd\Documents\0_AAdocs\7_Project_-_DataGen"

In that main directory are several subdirectories:

"data_config" subdirectory holds a configuration file (config_main.ini) that all other python scripts use to locate other files and directories.  This file is in the form used by the python library "configparser".

"data_results" subdirectory holds output CSV files generated by python scripts.  This subdirectory holds three more subdirectories:

- "base" holds newly generated CSV files
- "delta" holds later updates to the generated CSV files
- "merged" holds combined CSV files from "base" and "delta"

"python_datagen" subdirectory holds the python scripts that do the work of the application.

"DataGen_1010_Main_DataGen_Initial.py" is the master script that calls all the other scripts.

"DataGen_1110_Load_config_main.py" is a script that loads up the "config_main.ini" file and stores values in variables and arrays for use by all subsequent python scripts.

The remaining scripts are called in sequence from the "DataGen_1010_Main_DataGen_Initial.py" script:

DataGen_1210_Generate_items_initial.py
DataGen_1220_Generate_locations_initial.py
DataGen_1230_Generate_matrix_initial.py
DataGen_1240_Generate_coredata_initial.py
DataGen_1250_PostProcess_coredata.py

Using the library "tkinter" or "PySimpleGUI" create a script that will build a simple python-based user interface in the subdirectory ""C:\Users\AllanBarnard\OneDrive - Accelalpha Software Pvt. Ltd\Documents\0_AAdocs\7_Project_-_DataGen\python-ui" that will present a title of "DataGen" and three buttons:

Button #1: Edit the "config_main.ini" file

Button #2: Launch the python script "DataGen_1010_Main_DataGen_Initial.py"

Button #3: Exit



NOTE:
Install PySimpleGUI with:
python -m pip install --upgrade --extra-index-url https://PySimpleGUI.net/install PySimpleGUI
